goal: Clone one Franka Panda articulation into a 4x4 grid (16 envs, 2m spacing) for batched RL rollouts, preserving per-clone ArticulationRootAPI and physics stability.
thoughts: Maya wants GridCloner, not a Python for-loop — IsaacLab RL training expects the cloner's env registry because PhysX tensor views (ArticulationView) pattern-match on each env's Franka root. Steps: (1) ensure one source Franka is authored at /World/Franka_src via isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf or the manipulators package; (2) call clone_envs (or GridCloner.clone under run_usd_script) to produce /World/envs/env_{i}/Franka with the articulation root preserved; (3) verify_import on env_0/Franka to confirm ArticulationRootAPI stuck; (4) scene_summary / snapshot to count 16 roots; (5) sanity-nudge two clones' joint targets to confirm independent control. Pitfall: moving ArticulationRootAPI to /World/envs (a shared parent) breaks ArticulationView — PhysX cannot aggregate across a non-root marker. Pitfall: GridCloner.clone requires the source prim to be defined before clone() is called and the base_env_path created via Usd.Stage.DefinePrim before handing to the cloner.
code:
# 1. Seed the source robot (skip if Franka already at /World/Franka_src)
import_robot(
    asset_name="Franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka_src",
    fix_base=True
)

# 2. Create the env template and clone with GridCloner
run_usd_script(script="""
import omni.usd
from pxr import Usd, UsdGeom, Sdf
from isaacsim.core.cloner import GridCloner

stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()

# Parent group for all envs
UsdGeom.Xform.Define(stage, '/World/envs')
# Env-0 template; Cloner will duplicate its contents 15 more times
env_0 = UsdGeom.Xform.Define(stage, '/World/envs/env_0')

# Move the source Franka under env_0 as a reference (so each clone owns its root)
src_prim = stage.GetPrimAtPath('/World/Franka_src')
target = stage.DefinePrim('/World/envs/env_0/Franka', 'Xform')
target.GetReferences().AddInternalReference('/World/Franka_src')

cloner = GridCloner(spacing=2.0)
cloner.define_base_env(base_env_path='/World/envs/env_0')
target_paths = cloner.generate_paths('/World/envs/env', 16)  # env_0..env_15
cloner.clone(
    source_prim_path='/World/envs/env_0',
    prim_paths=target_paths,
    replicate_physics=True,  # critical: otherwise only env_0 has PhysX state
    copy_from_source=True
)
print('cloned', len(target_paths), 'envs')
""")

# 3. Verify one clone preserved its ArticulationRootAPI
verify_import(prim_path="/World/envs/env_7/Franka")

# 4. Snapshot the scene to confirm 16 Franka roots
scene_summary()

# 5. Independent-control sanity: nudge env_3 and env_11 different amounts
run_usd_script(script="""
from isaacsim.core.prims import Articulation
import numpy as np
for env_idx, target_deg in [(3, 10.0), (11, -10.0)]:
    art = Articulation(prim_paths_expr=f'/World/envs/env_{env_idx}/Franka')
    art.initialize()
    joints = art.get_joint_positions()[0]
    joints[0] = np.deg2rad(target_deg)
    art.set_joint_position_targets(joints[None, :])
    print(f'env_{env_idx} target set to {target_deg} deg on joint 0')
""")
failure_modes: Using a Python for-loop with create_prim/add_reference instead of GridCloner — breaks IsaacLab's ArticulationView batching
Moving ArticulationRootAPI to a shared parent like /World/envs — PhysX tensor view fails to aggregate; articulation reads return garbage
Forgetting replicate_physics=True on GridCloner.clone — only env_0 gets physics authoring, rest are visual-only
Defining env_0 AFTER calling define_base_env — cloner reads an empty prim and clones nothing
Hardcoding absolute positions in a loop instead of letting GridCloner manage spacing — grid layout drifts, and RL harness loses env-index → position mapping
Importing deprecated omni.isaac.cloner instead of isaacsim.core.cloner — ModuleNotFoundError on 5.x
